signal_title: Privacy and Other Legislation Amendment Bill 2024
risk_rationale: The draft bill requires the development of a Children's Online Privacy Code, which may introduce new privacy requirements for minors that are not currently covered in the baseline. The exact requirements are not specified yet, indicating a moderate risk that can be resolved within a quarter once the code is developed.

compliance_gaps.regulatory_requirement: Potential new privacy requirements for children under the Children's Online Privacy Code.
compliance_gaps.gap_description: The new code may introduce additional privacy requirements for minors that are not currently covered.

compliance_gaps.baseline_requirement: Account privacy for 13-15 years is private by default with no opt-out allowed.
compliance_gaps.regulatory_requirement: Potential new privacy requirements for children under the Children's Online Privacy Code.
compliance_gaps.gap_description: The new code may require changes to account privacy settings for minors.

remediations.title: Prepare for Children's Online Privacy Code
remediations.description: Monitor the development of the Children's Online Privacy Code and prepare to adjust privacy settings and policies for minors as required by the new code.
acceptance_criteria: Monitor OAIC announcements for the release of the Children's Online Privacy Code.
Develop a plan to adjust privacy settings for minors in compliance with the new code.

business_impact: Potential need to adjust privacy settings and policies for minors, which may affect user experience and require development resources.
recommended_actions: Monitor OAIC announcements for updates on the Children's Online Privacy Code.
Engage with OAIC during the code development process to provide input and understand potential impacts.
Prepare internal teams for potential changes to privacy settings and policies for minors.
